prompt_text: Open source or affordable AI tooling tools instead of Haystack
response:
If you want **open-source or affordable alternatives to Haystack**, the best-known options are **LlamaIndex**, **LangChain**, and, for lighter orchestration or agent workflows, **Semantic Kernel**; all are widely used frameworks for building LLM apps and RAG-style systems.[1][4]

A practical comparison:

| Tool | Best fit | Cost / openness |
|---|---|---|
| **LlamaIndex** | Production RAG and document retrieval pipelines | Open source[1][4] |
| **LangChain** | Flexible app orchestration and tool calling | Open source[1] |
| **Semantic Kernel** | Agent framework / developer SDK | Open source, MIT license[4] |
| **Haystack** | Production-ready RAG and agents | Open source[2] |

If you want something **cheaper than a hosted platform**, the source list also points to **local and BYOK tools** such as **Continue.dev**, **Cline**, **Aider**, **Open WebUI**, **Ollama**, **LM Studio**, **ChromaDB**, and **LanceDB** for building an inexpensive self-hosted stack.[1]

For a simple decision:
- Choose **LlamaIndex** if your main need is **RAG over documents**.[1][4]
- Choose **LangChain** if you need **more general orchestration and integrations**.[1]
- Choose **Semantic Kernel** if you want a **framework-style SDK** with a lighter developer focus.[4]
- Choose **Continue.dev / Cline / Aider + Ollama** if you want an **affordable local coding or agent workflow**.[1]

If you want, I can also give you a **Haystack vs LlamaIndex vs LangChain** comparison specifically for **RAG, agents, and production deployment**.
